title: Lvalues not recognized properly in classes deriving from their own parameters
body:
Minimal reproduction example:
```SystemVerilog
class Foo;
endclass

class Bar #(type BASE=Foo) extends BASE;
  task body();
    int v = 0;
    v = 1;
  endtask
endclass
```

Ran with 
```
$ verilator --lint-only bad.v --debug
```
Result:
```
%Error: Internal Error: bad.v:7:7: ../V3Broken.cpp:225: Assignment LHS is not an lvalue
-node: ASSIGN 0x555556476f30 <e627> {d7ah} @dt=0x5555564768d0@(G/sw32)
    7 |     v = 1;
      |       ^
                        ... See the manual at https://verilator.org/verilator_doc.html for more assistance.
```

Verilator version: a10b51705f8ed2634608097f8be1be96c4a1532b
OS: Gentoo Linux (x86_64)
